rag_rules: Place range assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annex expressions.
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and their usage in expressions.
Do not encode AADL property references as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
Generate minimal repairs that align with existing AADL scope and type rules.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses follow the pattern: assume "label": expression; guarantee "label": expression; eq name : type = expression; const name : type = value; assign output = expression;
anti_patterns: Do not reference package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
